message:
perf(storage): drop redundant idx_messages_conv_idx, revert append short-circuit

The `messages` table already declares `UNIQUE(conversation_id, idx)`, which
SQLite implements with an autoindex covering exactly the same key as the
explicit named index `idx_messages_conv_idx`. Maintaining both meant every
message insert paid for two index updates with identical lookup capability.

Migration v15 (`drop_redundant_messages_conv_idx`) drops the explicit index
in place; fresh schemas no longer create it. Lookup/order semantics are
preserved end-to-end via the UNIQUE constraint's autoindex, so query plans
that previously hit `idx_messages_conv_idx` transparently fall through to
the autoindex with the same cost.

Also reverts the noop short-circuit branches added in d9ed60d0 across
`append_existing_conversation_in_tx`, `franken_*` append paths, and the
`ended_at` MAX update guard. With the duplicate index gone, the wasted
write work the short-circuit was trying to dodge no longer dominates, and
keeping the conversation `ended_at` advance unconditional is required so
last-activity timestamps stay correct when a conversation is re-ingested
with the same set of messages but a newer tail timestamp on the source
side. The accompanying `append_existing_conversation_noop_skips_append_tail_write_work`
test is removed since the behavior it asserted is intentionally gone.

Bumps CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 14 → 15 and registers the migration in both
`build_cass_migrations` and `MIGRATION_NAMES`.
